title: 2.1 Characteristics of Plants
description: Characteristics of plants — every fact from the textbook.

questions.question: Which statement describes plants as living things?
options: Plants grow, reproduce and respond to changes in the environment
questions.correctAnswer: Plants grow, reproduce and respond to changes in the environment

questions.question: What type of cells make up plants?
What structures do plant cells have that are well-defined?
What is the rigid cell wall of plant cells primarily made of?
Besides the cell membrane, what additional structure do plant cells have?
What does the term autotrophic mean in relation to plants?
Which green pigment enables plants to absorb sunlight and produce their own food?
What other name are plants called because they produce their own food?
What does it mean that plants are sessile?
options: They cannot move by themselves and remain fixed at one place
questions.correctAnswer: They cannot move by themselves and remain fixed at one place

questions.question: How are plants firmly anchored to the soil?
What response can the leaves of plants show?
options: Turn towards light and some respond to touch
questions.correctAnswer: Turn towards light and some respond to touch

questions.question: What can the roots of plants orient towards?
Which reproduction patterns do plants practice?
What is the dominant form of reproduction in lower plants such as mosses and liverworts?
In which group of plants is sexual reproduction the dominant and visible form?
What does sexual reproduction in higher plants involve?
options: The union of gametes or sex cells
questions.correctAnswer: The union of gametes or sex cells
